87	// ─── Debug info structures ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
88	
89	/// Debug introspection record.
90	///
91	/// C: `lua_Debug` in `lua.h`. Full mapping deferred to Phase E; this struct
92	/// holds only the fields that `ldebug.c` writes/reads.
93	///
94	/// # Port note
95	/// `name` and `namewhat` are optional byte strings because in C they can be
96	/// NULL. `source` is owned here because we build it from Proto.source (a GcRef).
97	/// `short_src` matches C layout as a fixed array.
98	pub struct LuaDebug {
99	    // C: int event
100	    pub event: i32,
101	    // C: const char *name  — (n) variable/function name
102	    pub name: Option<Vec<u8>>,
103	    // C: const char *namewhat  — (n) "global"/"local"/etc.
104	    pub namewhat: Option<&'static [u8]>,
105	    // C: const char *what  — (S) "Lua"/"C"/"main"
106	    pub what: Option<&'static [u8]>,
107	    // C: const char *source  — (S) source chunk name (raw bytes)
108	    pub source: Option<Vec<u8>>,
109	    // C: size_t srclen
110	    pub srclen: usize,
111	    // C: int currentline  — (l)
112	    pub currentline: i32,
113	    // C: int linedefined  — (S)
114	    pub linedefined: i32,
115	    // C: int lastlinedefined  — (S)
116	    pub lastlinedefined: i32,
117	    // C: unsigned char nups  — (u) number of upvalues
118	    pub nups: u8,
119	    // C: unsigned char nparams  — (u)
120	    pub nparams: u8,
121	    // C: char isvararg  — (u)
122	    pub isvararg: bool,
123	    // C: char istailcall  — (t)
124	    pub istailcall: bool,
125	    // C: unsigned short ftransfer / ntransfer  — (r)
126	    pub ftransfer: u16,
127	    pub ntransfer: u16,
128	    // C: char short_src[LUA_IDSIZE]  — (S) truncated source id
129	    pub short_src: [u8; LUA_IDSIZE],
130	    // C: struct CallInfo *i_ci  — private; the active CallInfo
131	    // PORT NOTE: C stores a raw pointer; Rust stores an index into LuaState.call_stack.
132	    pub i_ci: Option<CallInfoIdx>,
133	}
134	
135	impl Default for LuaDebug {
136	    fn default() -> Self {
137	        LuaDebug {
138	            event: 0,
139	            name: None,
140	            namewhat: None,
141	            what: None,
142	            source: None,
143	            srclen: 0,
144	            currentline: -1,
145	            linedefined: -1,
146	            lastlinedefined: -1,
147	            nups: 0,
148	            nparams: 0,
149	            isvararg: false,
150	            istailcall: false,
151	            ftransfer: 0,
152	            ntransfer: 0,
153	            short_src: [0u8; LUA_IDSIZE],
154	            i_ci: None,
155	        }
156	    }
157	}
158	
159	// ─── File-local helper: is this a Lua (non-C) closure? ───────────────────────
160	
161	// C: #define LuaClosure(f) ((f) != NULL && (f)->c.tt == LUA_VLCL)
162	// macros.tsv: LUA_VLCL → LuaClosure::Lua(_)
163	#[inline]
164	fn is_lua_closure(cl: Option<&LuaClosure>) -> bool {
165	    // C: (f) != NULL && (f)->c.tt == LUA_VLCL
166	    matches!(cl, Some(LuaClosure::Lua(_)))
167	}
168	
169	// ─── Current-PC helpers ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
170	
171	/// Returns the program counter (0-based instruction index) for the current
172	/// instruction in call frame `ci`.
173	///
174	/// C: `static int currentpc(CallInfo *ci)`
175	/// ```c
176	/// lua_assert(isLua(ci));
177	/// return pcRel(ci->u.l.savedpc, ci_func(ci)->p);
178	/// ```
179	///
180	/// PORT NOTE: In C, `savedpc` is a pointer to the *next* instruction. `pcRel`
181	/// subtracts the code base and then subtracts 1 more to get the *current*
182	/// instruction. In Rust, `saved_pc()` stores the 0-based index of the next
183	/// instruction, so the current instruction index is `saved_pc() - 1`.
184	fn current_pc(ci: &CallInfo) -> i32 {
185	    // C: lua_assert(isLua(ci))
186	    debug_assert!(ci.is_lua());
187	    // C: pcRel(ci->u.l.savedpc, ci_func(ci)->p)
188	    // macros.tsv: pcRel → (pc - proto.code_base()) as i32 - 1
189	    // In Rust savedpc is a u32 offset into code[]; current = savedpc - 1
190	    ci.saved_pc().saturating_sub(1) as i32
191	}
192	
193	// ─── Line-info lookup ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
194	
195	/// Finds the "base line" entry in `f.abslineinfo` for instruction `pc`.
196	///
197	/// Sets `*basepc` to the pc of the base entry (or -1 if starting from the
198	/// function's first line), and returns the line number at that base.
199	///
200	/// C: `static int getbaseline(const Proto *f, int pc, int *basepc)`
201	fn get_baseline(f: &LuaProto, pc: i32, basepc: &mut i32) -> i32 {
202	    // C: if (f->sizeabslineinfo == 0 || pc < f->abslineinfo[0].pc)
203	    if f.abslineinfo.is_empty() || pc < f.abslineinfo[0].pc {
204	        // C: *basepc = -1; return f->linedefined;
205	        *basepc = -1;
206	        return f.linedefined;
207	    }
208	    // C: int i = cast_uint(pc) / MAXIWTHABS - 1;
209	    // macros.tsv: cast_uint(x) → x as u32
210	    let mut i = (pc as u32 / MAX_IWTH_ABS as u32).saturating_sub(1) as usize;
211	    // C: lua_assert(i < 0 || (i < f->sizeabslineinfo && f->abslineinfo[i].pc <= pc))
212	    debug_assert!(
213	        i < f.abslineinfo.len() && f.abslineinfo[i].pc <= pc,
214	        "getbaseline: estimate is not a lower bound"
215	    );
216	    // C: while (i + 1 < f->sizeabslineinfo && pc >= f->abslineinfo[i + 1].pc) i++;
217	    while i + 1 < f.abslineinfo.len() && pc >= f.abslineinfo[i + 1].pc {
218	        i += 1;
219	    }
220	    // C: *basepc = f->abslineinfo[i].pc; return f->abslineinfo[i].line;
221	    *basepc = f.abslineinfo[i].pc;
222	    f.abslineinfo[i].line
223	}
224	
225	/// Returns the source line number corresponding to instruction `pc` in proto `f`.
226	/// Returns -1 if the proto has no debug line information.
227	///
228	/// C: `int luaG_getfuncline(const Proto *f, int pc)` (LUAI_FUNC)
229	pub(crate) fn get_func_line(f: &LuaProto, pc: i32) -> i32 {

content.text:
**File**: `crates/lua-stdlib/src/state_stub.rs`, trait default at line 192 (target todo `get_stack`). All new code lives in the `impl LuaStateStubExt for LuaState` block (now ending around line ~1150).

**What `get_stack` does**: Delegates to `lua_vm::debug::get_stack` and stashes the resulting `CallInfoIdx` into a newly-added `LuaDebug.i_ci_idx` field so later `get_info`/`get_local_at` calls can resolve the frame (matches C's private `lua_Debug.i_ci` slot).

**Family members implemented (in-file, same impl block)**:
- `get_stack_level` — alias of `get_stack`.
- `get_info` — builds a `lua_vm::debug::LuaDebug` seeded with `i_ci_idx`, calls canonical `get_info`, then `copy_lvm_debug_to_stub` mirrors fields back (handles `what` byte ↔ `&[u8]` tag, `short_src` array↔Vec trim, `Option` flattening).
- `get_debug_info` — wraps `get_info`.
- `get_local_at` — bridges to `lua_vm::debug::get_local(Some(&lvm_ar), n)`.
- `set_local_at` — bridges to `lua_vm::debug::set_local`.
- `get_param_name` — `get_local(self, None, n)` (None-ar path reads param names from function on stack top).
- `has_frames` — `!state.is_base_ci(state.current_ci_idx())`.
- `lua_traceback` — delegates to `crate::auxlib::traceback`.

**Supporting change**: added private `i_ci_idx: Option<CallInfoIdx>` field to stub `LuaDebug` (file-local struct, `LuaDebug::default()` still works; no struct-literal callers); imported `CallInfoIdx` from `lua_types`. Added private `copy_lvm_debug_to_stub` helper.

**Newly-surfaced todos NOT implemented (different families)**: hook family (`set_hook_full`, `hook_is_set`, `hook_is_internal_lua_hook`, `get_hook_count`, `get_hook_mask`); warning family (`set_warn_fn`, `write_output`); `upvalue_id`, `join_upvalues`; `set_c_stack_limit`; `new_thread`, `is_same_thread`; `new_userdata_typed`, `get_iuservalue`, `set_iuservalue`.

**`cargo build -p lua-cli`**: green (warnings only, zero errors).
